rulings.Case Title: LEONARDA O. HIDALGO VS. SODABEH GOLDSTINAT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion In Support Of Motion To Dismiss The First Amended Complaint For Delay In Prosecution, Request Of Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 22, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANTS BARBARA JIMENEZ, CAPITAL ALLIANCE FUNDING CORPORATION, and, HILFASCO, INC. Motion To Dismiss The First Amended Complaint For Delay In Prosecution. Motion denied. The deaths of plaintiff and of her attorney resulted in difficulty prosecuting this matter. Reasonable excuse demonstrated for the delay and courts favor resolution on the merits. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 22, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFF BARBARA WILLIAMS Motion To Reconsider and Revoke Order Enforcing Settlement. Motion denied. No new or different facts presented to support motion for reconsideration. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J & L COLLECTIONS, INC., DBA J & L TEAMWORKS VS. LOIMATAFIA CARLOS-VALENTINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Leave To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 22, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANTS LOIMATAFIA CARLOS-VALENTINO, and, NICOLE CARLOS-VALENTINO Motion To Set Aside Default and Leave To Defend. Motion denied. On 4/10/08, after Solomon withdrew, plaintiff sent a letter to defendants warning them to answer. Additionally, defendants were re-served with the copies of the summons and complaint in August 2008 and did nothing. While Solomon may have contributed to the error, he was not solely at fault. (See Lang vs. Hochman (2000) 77 Cal.App. 4th 1225.) Defendants should have taken some affirmative steps to defend themselves. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 22, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion off calendar, in view of ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 22, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to first cause of action. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting an agreement and transaction that occurred between plaintiff and defendant. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for fraud to plead with specificity, plead reliance and intent to deceive. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for breach of express warranty and fifth cause of action for breach of implied warranty. Latent defects that are discovered after expiration of the express warranty are not covered. (Daugherty vs. American Honda (2006) 144 Cal.App. 4th 824.) No vertical privity to support implied warranty, or allegation Pathfinder was purchased new. Demurrer overruled as to second cause of action for B&P Code section 17200 claims and sixth cause of action for unjust enrichment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JEFF BREIBART VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY & COUNTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Special Motion To Strike Slapp Suit
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 22, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and, MATTHEW ROTHSCHILD Special Motion To Strike Slapp Suit. Motion denied. Defendant fails to make a prima facie showing of protected activity. No showing e-mail to MUNI concerned an issue under consideration by a public entity. =(302/CWW)
